title: Permission to Feel: Unlocking the Power of Emotions to Help Our Kids, Ourselves, and Our Society Thrive
subtitle: The RULER system for emotional literacy — a research-based approach to recognizing, understanding, labeling, expressing, and regulating emotions.
author: Marc Brackett
themes: The RULER framework: Recognize, Understand, Label, Express, Regulate
Emotional literacy as a teachable skill, not an innate trait
The role of emotions in learning, decision making, and relationships
Why permission to feel is the foundation of emotional intelligence
How to create emotionally safe environments at home and school
awards: Best Book of 2019 — Greater Good Science Center
Founding Director, Yale Center for Emotional Intelligence
excerpt: Marc Brackett, founding director of the Yale Center for Emotional Intelligence, argues that the most important skill we can teach our children — and ourselves — is emotional literacy. The RULER system (Recognize, Understand, Label, Express, Regulate) is a research-based framework for developing emotional skills. Brackett shows how giving ourselves and others permission to feel transforms schools, workplaces, and families.
